title: Doors
metaDescription: Door widening, sliding, pocket, barn, and automated doors for home accessibility. Aging-In-Place Remodeling modifies doorways for wheelchair and mobility access.
intro: Doors very often present a huge barrier to our clients. Over many years, we've modified hundreds of doorways. Common problems include insufficient width for access, doors blocking pathways, difficult handles/operation, and rough movement.

subServices.name: Door Widening
subServices.description: The most frequently requested service. Older homes typically have 24-30" doorways, but wheelchairs require 26" minimum. We install doors reaching at least 36" or wider when structurally feasible.

subServices.name: Sliding/Pocket/Barn Doors
subServices.description: Replace traditional swinging doors that create mobility obstacles. Linear-path doors eliminate the large swing radius and dexterity demands.

subServices.name: Exterior Doors
subServices.description: ADA-compliant exterior doors from partner manufacturers. Work effectively combined with custom ramp solutions for outdoor accessibility.

subServices.name: Automated Doors
subServices.description: Push-button door openers providing user independence. Installable on interior/exterior swinging doors and gates within hours. We are dealers for Open Sesame Door Openers.
